rulings.Case Title: JOHN PAPPAGEORGE VS. MARTIN V MURPHY JR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Additional Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 18, 2014, Line 1: PLAINTIFF JOHN PAPPAGEORGE'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Additional Monetary Sanctions: Hearing Required. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MINHALL, INC.A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. RICHARD A. CHRISTENSEN, AN INDIVIDUAL, DOING et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 18, 2014, Line 2: PLAINTIFF MINHALL, INC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/Adjudication: DENIED. Moving party shifted its burden. Opposing party presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue of fact regarding whether reduced rent was accepted in full satisfaction of monthly rent satisfaction or as a partial rent. Motion for summary adjudication is denied. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ALENE YOUNG VS. NANCY WILLIAMS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Discovery And Awarding Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 18, 2014, Line 3: PLAINTIFF ALENE YOUNG's Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Discovery And Awarding Sanctions: GRANTED, responses to Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories and Request for Production without objections must be provided to Plaintiff within 10 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $375. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: FRED MCNEAL VS. JILL LUCKHUST, INDIVIDUALLY AND IN HER CAPACITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 18, 2014, Line 4: DEFENDANTS JILL LUCKHURST and CHERYL BREWSTER's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT: continued to December 18, 2014 at 9:30 AM in Department 501. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B and C and provide courtesy copies of the reply and the operative complaint to department 501 no later than November 25, 2014 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. Opposing party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6CB and provide courtesy copy of opposition to department 501 no later than November 25, 2014 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KIMBERLY K.A. CHIN ET AL VS. SANDRA WONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Regarding Order To Show Cause
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 18, 2014, Line 5: PLAINTIFFS KIMBERLY CHIN's Motion Regarding Order To Show Cause: Denied. Moving party failed to sustain burden. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KIMBERLY K.A. CHIN ET AL VS. SANDRA WONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 18, 2014, Line 5: DEFENDANTS EQUITY GROWTH ASSET MANAGEMENT and FOUNDERS REALTY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: Sustained with leave to amend to allege facts showing that Equity is not a bona fide purchaser or to allege a cause of action for quiet title against a bona fide purchaser. Demurrer is overruled on SOL and holder of legal title grounds. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KIMBERLY K.A. CHIN ET AL VS. SANDRA WONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 18, 2014, Line 5: DEFENDANT OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: Sustained. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the following causes of action: with leave to amend: (1) fraud, wrongful foreclosure, and B&P 17200 is sustained with leave to amend to allege fraud with requisite particularity as to moving defendant and to allege all elements of B&P 17200 cause of action; (2) conversion - to allege the amount allegedly converted by the moving defendant for defendant's own use; (3) good faith and fair dealing - to allege all elements of this cause of action; (4) breach of fiduciary duty and negligence - to allege duty; (5) Rosenthal Act-sustained with leave to amend to alleged fact re: Old Republic being a debt collector within the meaning of the Act; (6) injunctive relief- to re-plead as a remedy. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to quiet title cause of action, no opposition filed. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: B&B KEITH DEVELOPMENT, LLC VS. PETER LA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 18, 2014, Line 6: DEFENDANTS PETER LA and MICHAEL MUCTHISON'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND: DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden. Proof of Personal Service by a registered process server on file. The same process server severed Summons and original complaint. Plaintiff and its counsel are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B in future. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MISSION BAY HOUSING PARTNERS, LP VS. STEPHANIE A PULVIRENTI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 18, 2014, Line 7: DEFENDANT STEPHANIE PULVIRENTI'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT: OFF CALENDAR. Moving party failed to comply with SFLR 2.6B and Court's November 10, 2014 minute order. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: NANCY Y WILLLIAMS VS. ALENE YOUNG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 18, 2014, Line 8: DEFENDANT ALENE YOUNG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: Sustained with leave to amend to address all issues raised by demurrer. Opposing party failed to comply with SFLR 2.6B even after the matter was continued from November 4, 2014 to give plaintiff an opportunity to comply. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BAY PROPERTY GROUP VS. ABENET TEKIE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Defendant Ariel Bentolila'S Specially Prepared Interrogatories, Set One, To Bay Property Group; For Sanctions Of Up To $6,800.00 Against Jonathan Bornstein
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 18, 2014, Line 9: DEFENDANT ARIEL BANTOLILA'S Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Defendant Ariel Bentolila'S Specially Prepared Interrogatories, Set One, To Bay Property Group; For Sanctions Of Up To $6,800.00 Against Jonathan Bornstein: Continued to November 20, 2014 to be heard in department 302 at 9:30 a.m. (501/REQ)
